title: Cross-adapter parity harness: same workload runs identically on WSL2Podman / WindowsNativeJail / Docker
goal: Build the canonical cross-adapter parity harness that runs a fixed workload matrix against all three SandboxAdapter implementations and asserts identical observable outcomes (exit code, stdout content, ProcessOwnershipLedger row shape modulo timestamps + UUIDs, NetPolicy enforcement behavior). Closes AC-SANDBOX-ADAPTER-IMPLS for the cluster — proves the 3 adapters are interchangeable at the trait surface for the workloads cluster C and cluster D depend on.
expected_diff_shape: New tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/sandbox_cross_adapter_parity_tests.rs running the same workload set against each available adapter; new harness module src/backend/handshake_core/src/test_harness/cross_adapter.rs that holds the fixture catalog and parity-comparator. Test fixture catalog under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fixtures/cross_adapter_parity/{trivial_exit.json, stdout_capture.json, bind_readonly.json, net_deny_all.json, net_loopback_only.json, env_passthrough.json, kill_signal_term.json, kill_signal_int.json}.
unblock_conditions: All three adapters + bootstrap + model-process boxing + ProcessOwnershipLedger merged so parity comparison can include ledger-row shape.
implementation_notes: Harness shape: `pub struct CrossAdapterParityHarness { fixtures: Vec<ParityFixture>, adapters: Vec<Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>> }` where `ParityFixture { name, process_spec_builder: fn(&Path) -> ProcessSpec, expected_outcome: ExpectedOutcome }` and `ExpectedOutcome { exit_code_class: ExitClass(Zero|NonZero), stdout_predicate: StdoutPredicate(ExactBytes|Substring|MatchesRegex), enforce_net_policy: bool, enforce_bind_readonly: bool }`. The harness iterates fixtures and runs each against each adapter in `adapters`; for each (fixture, adapter) pair it: (1) calls adapter.spawn(spec), (2) collects stdout via adapter.exec(handle, get_stdout_cmd) OR via the spawn-stream depending on adapter capabilities, (3) waits for exit via polling adapter.status, (4) reads exit_code, (5) verifies the expected outcome predicates, (6) reads the kernel_process_lifecycle row written by the LedgerDecorator (MT-053) and asserts the shape (modulo UUIDs/timestamps) matches across adapters. Parity assertion: across the 3 adapters, the (exit_code, stdout_bytes, ledger_row_shape) tuple MUST be identical modulo (UUIDs, timestamps, adapter_id, sandbox_internal_id). Skipping discipline: when a real adapter backend is unavailable (no WSL2, no Docker), that adapter is skipped with `#[ignore]`-style notice but never causes false-pass — the harness records `skipped_adapters: Vec<AdapterId>` in the test output so the validator sees coverage gaps explicitly. Fixture details: trivial_exit (echo hello, exit 0); stdout_capture (echo specific multi-line string, byte-exact comparison); bind_readonly (touch a file in a ReadOnly bind, expect EROFS-class exit code AND ledger row with non-zero exit); net_deny_all (getent hosts cnn.com, expect non-zero exit + no DNS resolution); net_loopback_only (spin a tiny server in another container? — skip if cross-adapter cross-container is intractable; instead do a `curl http://127.0.0.1:0` smoke against a `nc -l` started in the same container via separate exec); env_passthrough (env var set in ProcessSpec is visible in `printenv` output); kill_signal_term (long-running `sleep 60`, kill Term, expect exit within 10s); kill_signal_int (`trap 'exit 42' INT; sleep 60`, kill Int, expect exit code 42).
risk_if_missed: Without cross-adapter parity, the 3 adapters drift in behavior (NetPolicy semantic skew, BindMode semantic skew) and cluster C ModelRuntime adapters silently work on one but break on another.
validator_focus: (a) skipped_adapters is reported (not silently passed); (b) parity assertion modulo UUIDs/timestamps holds for every fixture that ran; (c) net_deny_all and bind_readonly fixtures verify enforcement, not just absence-of-error.

minimum_controls: skipped_adapters reporting prevents false-pass when a CI host lacks one of the three backends.
Parity assertion is strict on (exit_code, stdout_bytes, ledger_row_shape) modulo the documented allowed-drift fields.
Enforcement fixtures (net_deny_all, bind_readonly) test for OS-level enforcement, not just trait-method success.
